data.title: Lundin Mining forecasts 2026 copper output 310–335K tonnes, gold 134–149K ounces
data.description:
• Lundin Mining (LUN:CA) projects consolidated copper production of 310,000–335,000 tonnes and gold output of 134,000–149,000 ounces in 2026, with cash costs expected between $1.90/lb and $2.10/lb.
  • Forecasted consolidated copper production of 315,000 to 340,000 tonnes in 2027 and 290,000 to 315,000 tonnes in 2028.
  • Sustaining capital expenditures of $550 million and expansionary capital expenditures of $445 million in 2026.
  • Exploration expenditures are forecast to be $53 million, primarily for in-mine and near-mine targets in 2026.
  • The company exceeded its original full-year consolidated copper production guidance and was within the increased full-year production guidance for 2025. Strong operational performance, particularly at Caserones, drove production growth for the year.
  • Candelaria produced 145,471 tonnes of copper for the full year, benefiting from higher mill throughput due to softer ore.

data.title: Europe indexes rebound after Trump's reversal on Greenland
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.68% to 10,209. UK public sector net borrowing fell to £11.6B in December.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.24% to 24,865. 

France (CAC:IND) +1.33% to 8,176, France’s 10-year OAT yield hovered around 3.53%, rebounding from 3.48% on January 21, as investors stayed on edge amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

In other parts of Europe, Norway’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate fell to 4.3% in December 2025.

Denmark’s consumer confidence index increased to -13.4 in January 2026.

Consumer confidence in the Netherlands declined to -23 in January 2026.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 1.13% higher around €609.5, recovering losses from earlier losses seen this week after U.S. President Donald Trump abandoned plans to impose tariffs on several European countries and ruled out using military force to acquire Greenland. 

Besides, gold hovered around $4,830 per ounce, erasing earlier losses to remain close to a record high.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 1 basis point to 4.24%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 2.88%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.43%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Elliott raises stake in Toyota Industries, seeks better terms for privatization deal
data.description:
• Elliott Investment Management revealed it has increased its stake in Toyota Industries Corp. (TYIDY) as the activist fund seeks a more favorable deal in the Toyota group’s plan to take the company private.
  • The investor now has a 6.7% stake in Toyota Industries, up from 5% in November, according to a filing on Thursday. That makes Elliott the second-biggest shareholder in Toyota Industries, after Toyota Motor Corp. (TM) and its affiliates.
  • The activist fund is calling on other minority shareholders to reject the proposal and push for a higher bid.
  • Last week, Toyota Motor (TM) raised the buyout offer to minority shareholders of Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF) to 18,800 yen per share, from 16,300 yen apiece, after investor pushback.
  • Of note, Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF) is Japan’s largest forklift producer and a major materials-handling player, and it also makes engines and components for key Toyota-branded vehicles such as the RAV4, Corolla, Crown, and Land Cruiser.

data.title: Tryg A/S reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Tryg A/S press release (TGVSF): Q4 Insurance revenue growth of 4.1% in local currencies (3.6%).
  • Insurance service result of DKK 1,918m (DKK 1,708m).
  • Combined ratio of 81.4% (82.5%).
  • Expense ratio of 13.6% (13.3%).
  • Investment result of DKK 171m (DKK -265m).
  • Profit before tax of DKK 1,707m (DKK 1,033m).
  • Ordinary dividend of DKK 2.05 (DKK 1.95).

data.title: Investor AB GAAP EPS of SEK 18.09, revenue of SEK 16.79B
data.description:
• Investor AB press release (IVSXF): Q4 GAAP EPS of SEK 18.09.
  • Revenue of SEK 16.79B (+2.4% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted net asset value amounted to SEK 1,087.1bn (SEK 355 per share) on December 31, 2025, a change of SEK 59.1bn, or 6 percent, with dividend added back, during the quarter.
  • Leverage was 2.1 percent as of December 31, 2025 (1.2 percent as of December 31, 2024). Gross cash amounted to SEK 27,119m and the average maturity of Investor AB’s debt portfolio was 9.2 years.
  • For fiscal year 2025, the Board of Directors proposes a dividend per share of SEK 5.60 (5.20) to be paid in two installments, SEK 4.00 in May, 2026, and SEK 1.60 in November, 2026.

data.title: EQT AB Non-GAAP EPS of €1.12, revenue of €2.73B
data.description:
• EQT AB (publ) press release (EQBBF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €1.12.
  • Revenue of €2.73B (+3.0% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA amounted to €1,642 m ( €1,359 m), corresponding to an EBITDA margin of 60 % (58%).
  • The Board proposes a dividend to the shareholders of SEK 5.00 (4.30) per share for the fiscal year 2025. The dividend is proposed to be paid out in two installments, SEK 2.50 (2.15) in May 2026 and SEK 2.50 (2.15) in December 2026.
  • Gross inflows amounted to € 26bn – more than double the € 11bn of inflows in 2024 . FAUM increased to € 141bn (€ 136bn).
  • Total AUM was € 270bn ( € 269bn) at the end of the period.

data.title: Unit prices upsized senior notes offering of $1B
data.description:
• Uniti Group (UNIT) priced an offering of $1B aggregate principal amount of 8.625% senior notes due 2032.
  • The offering was upsized by $500M aggregate principal amount compared to the previously announced offering size of $500M.
  • Net proceeds will repay borrowings under Uniti Services' senior secured first lien term loan due 2031, covering fees and expenses, with remaining funds for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 4, 2026.

data.title: Bankinter, S.A. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Bankinter, S.A. press release (BKIMF): Q4 Revenue of €569.99M (+3.6% Y/Y).
  • Return on equity stood at a record 18.9%, one percentage point higher than the same ratio last year, with ROTE at 20%.
  • Meanwhile, the CET1 capital ratio ended the period at 12.72%.
  • In terms of efficiency, the institution continued to lead its peers with a cost-toincome ratio of 36.1%, marking a year-on-year improvement of 23 basis points.
  • Looking at asset quality, the NPL ratio fell by 17 basis points compared with 2024, to 1.94%, with a very robust coverage ratio of 68%.

data.title: Resolute Mining reports Q4 results; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Resolute Mining press release (RMGGF): Q4 Group gold production of 65,918 ounces (oz) (Q3 2025: 59,857oz) meeting expectations due to continued stockpile processing at Mako (Senegal) and improved underground output at Syama (Mali).
  • All-In Sustaining Costs of $1,877/oz (Q3 2025: $2,205/oz) in line with expectations as higher gold production partially offset higher royalty payments.
  • Quarterly capital expenditure (excluding exploration) of $18.4 million (Q3 2025: $26.6 million) in line with expectations consisting of $16.0 million non-sustaining and $2.4 million of sustaining capital.
  • Q4 operating cash flow generation of $85.7 million (Q3: $67.8 million) (operating cash flow before capital expenditure, exploration and working capital).

data.title: Thailand’s Gulf Development teams up with Google to explore AI opportunities
data.description:
• Billionaire Sarath Ratanavadi’s Gulf Development Pcl, Thailand’s top power producer, is expanding into artificial intelligence through a new partnership with Alphabet Inc.’s Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to explore joint business ventures.
  • Gulf Edge Co., Gulf Development’s unit, signed a strategic framework agreement with Google to provide AI infrastructure services, according to a Thai exchange filing.
  • The partnership aims to support Gulf Development’s transition into an AI-native technology company, it said.
  • The collaboration also aims to launch commercial services for enterprise (B2B) and consumer (B2C) engagement by leveraging Google’s Gemini models while simultaneously modernizing business processes through agentic AI.
